source_paper_title: Semidefinite Approximations for Bicliques and Bi-Independent Pairs

Section 7 (Concluding remarks), page 34.

Hence, finding a stronger closed-form bound for \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\) that is able to take advantage of the restriction to balanced independent sets remains a problem.
title: Find stronger closed-form eigenvalue bounds for balanced independent sets in regular bipartite graphs
background_latex:
Let $G=(V_1\cup V_2,E)$ be a bipartite graph. A pair $(A,B)$ with $A\subseteq V_1$ and $B\subseteq V_2$ is a bipartite biindependent pair if there is no edge between $A$ and $B$, i.e., $A\times B\cap E=\emptyset$. Such a pair is balanced if $|A|=|B|$.

Define the balanced independent-set size
\[
\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G):=\max\{\,|A|+|B|:\ (A,B)\text{ is a balanced bipartite biindependent pair in }G\,\}.
\]
Equivalently, $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)=2\max\{|A|:(A,B)\text{ balanced biindependent}\}$.

A basic spectral upper bound for certain biindependent-pair parameters is obtained as follows. Suppose $G$ is $r$-regular with $|V_1|=|V_2|=n$, and let $A_G\in\mathbb{R}^{2n\times 2n}$ be the adjacency matrix of $G$. Let $\lambda_2(A_G)$ denote the second largest eigenvalue of $A_G$ (equivalently, the second largest singular value of the $n\times n$ bipartite adjacency block).

The paper defines an eigenvalue-based bound
\[
\mathrm{b}h(G):=\frac{n\,\lambda_2(A_G)}{2\,(r+\lambda_2(A_G))},
\]
obtained by restricting certain semidefinite relaxations to highly symmetric feasible solutions. For bipartite regular graphs one has an inequality of the form
\[
\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\le 4\,\mathrm{b}h(G)
\]
whenever the relaxation used indeed upper bounds $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)$ (the paper studies several such balanced SDP relaxations and shows that their natural symmetric specializations collapse to $\mathrm{b}h(G)$).

The motivating issue is that $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)$ is NP-hard to compute, so efficiently computable closed-form spectral upper bounds (in terms of eigenvalues of standard matrices associated to $G$) are desirable, especially bounds that exploit the balancing constraint $|A|=|B|$ more sharply than existing ones.
problem_definition_latex:
Determine a closed-form eigenvalue-based upper bound on $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)$ for $r$-regular bipartite graphs $G=(V_1\cup V_2,E)$ (with $|V_1|=|V_2|=n$) that is provably stronger than the bound obtained from $\mathrm{b}h(G)=\frac{n\lambda_2(A_G)}{2(r+\lambda_2(A_G))}$, i.e., a bound that can be strictly smaller than $4\,\mathrm{b}h(G)$ while remaining valid for all such graphs.

Equivalently, find a tractable (spectral, closed-form) bound that better exploits the balancing requirement $|A|=|B|$ than the existing symmetric eigenvalue bound arising from the paper's balanced semidefinite relaxations, which all reduce (up to scaling) to $\mathrm{b}h(G)$ on regular bipartite graphs.
latex:
Let $G=(V_1\cup V_2,E)$ be a bipartite graph. A pair $(A,B)$ with $A\subseteq V_1$ and $B\subseteq V_2$ is a bipartite biindependent pair if there is no edge between $A$ and $B$, i.e., $A\times B\cap E=\emptyset$. Such a pair is balanced if $|A|=|B|$.

A basic spectral upper bound for certain biindependent-pair parameters is obtained as follows. Suppose $G$ is $r$-regular with $|V_1|=|V_2|=n$, and let $A_G\in\mathbb{R}^{2n\times 2n}$ be the adjacency matrix of $G$. Let $\lambda_2(A_G)$ denote the second largest eigenvalue of $A_G$ (equivalently, the second largest singular value of the $n\times n$ bipartite adjacency block).

The paper defines an eigenvalue-based bound
\[

\[

Determine a closed-form eigenvalue-based upper bound on $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)$ for $r$-regular bipartite graphs $G=(V_1\cup V_2,E)$ (with $|V_1|=|V_2|=n$) that is provably stronger than the bound obtained from $\mathrm{b}h(G)=\frac{n\lambda_2(A_G)}{2(r+\lambda_2(A_G))}$, i.e., a bound that can be strictly smaller than $4\,\mathrm{b}h(G)$ while remaining valid for all such graphs.

Equivalently, find a tractable (spectral, closed-form) bound that better exploits the balancing requirement $|A|=|B|$ than the existing symmetric eigenvalue bound arising from the paper's balanced semidefinite relaxations, which all reduce (up to scaling) to $\mathrm{b}h(G)$ on regular bipartite graphs.
open_problem_quote: Hence, finding a stronger closed-form bound for \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\) that is able to take advantage of the restriction to balanced independent sets remains a problem.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally search over small regular bipartite graphs and compare $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)$ (via ILP/branch-and-bound) against candidate spectral formulas to refute overly-strong conjectured bounds. However, the space of regular bipartite graphs grows quickly, and verifying universality of an inequality still requires broad coverage or theoretical arguments.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate closed-form bounds can be parameterized (e.g., as expressions involving several eigenvalues of $A_G$, Laplacian eigenvalues, or singular values of the bipartite adjacency block) and scored by the worst-case gap over a training set of generated regular bipartite graphs where $\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)$ is computed exactly for small sizes. An evolutionary loop can iteratively propose formulas/coefficients and optimize them against this computable fitness signal, potentially discovering improved conjectural bounds or extremal families.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The source paper of Laurent–Polak–Vargas (2024) formulates balanced bipartite biindependent pairs and the balanced independent-set size \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\), and develops several first-level SOS/SDP relaxations (balanced variants of Lovász’ \(\vartheta\)-number and related Lasserre bounds). For \(r\)-regular bipartite graphs with \(|V_1|=|V_2|=n\), the paper shows that when one restricts these SDPs to highly symmetric feasible solutions (the natural route to closed-form spectral bounds), all resulting eigenvalue expressions collapse (up to scaling) to the same \(\lambda_2\)-based quantity \(\mathrm{b}h(G)=\frac{n\lambda_2(A_G)}{2(r+\lambda_2(A_G))}\). In particular, the balanced SDPs do not yield a strictly smaller closed-form upper bound on \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\) than the inequality \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\le 4\,\mathrm{b}h(G)\) obtained via \(h\)-type relaxations.

At present (based on the provided material and absence of forward-citation evidence), the problem of finding a provably stronger universal closed-form eigenvalue bound exploiting \(|A|=|B|\) remains open. The closest related spectral frameworks are the Lovász theta method (Lovász 1979; Grötschel–Lovász–Schrijver 1988) and Hoffman/Haemers-style ratio and biclique bounds (Haemers 1997, 2001; Haemers 2021). These works suggest possible directions: (i) move beyond a \(\lambda_2\)-only formula by incorporating additional spectral data (e.g., multiple singular values/eigenvalue sums, Laplacian extremals, or interlacing constraints tied to the bipartition), and/or (ii) derive new dual feasible certificates for balanced SDPs that are not forced into the two-parameter symmetric ansatz \(Z=tA_G\) (and scalar diagonal shifts) that yields \(\mathrm{b}h(G)\).

Promising technical avenues include exploiting equitable partitions/quotient matrices induced by candidate balanced cuts, applying refined eigenvalue interlacing to the bipartite adjacency block \(M_G\) with constraints reflecting \(|A|=|B|\), or designing spectral bounds from higher-level SOS relaxations that still admit tractable symmetry reductions (e.g., association-scheme settings) but retain enough degrees of freedom to separate balanced from unbalanced solutions. Any such bound would need to be computable from standard spectra and demonstrably strictly smaller than \(4\,\mathrm{b}h(G)\) on some regular bipartite families while remaining valid for all.

sources.title: Semidefinite approximations for bicliques and biindependent pairs
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces SDP relaxations for bipartite biindependent-pair parameters and for the balanced parameter \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\). For \(r\)-regular bipartite \(G\) with \(|V_1|=|V_2|=n\), shows the closed-form spectral bound \(h(G)\le h_1(G)\le \mathrm{b}h(G)=\frac{n\lambda_2}{2(r+\lambda_2)}\) and that natural symmetric specializations of several balanced SDPs collapse (up to scaling) to \(\mathrm{b}h(G)\), leaving open the task of finding a strictly stronger closed-form eigenvalue bound exploiting balance.
sources.connection: This is the originating paper: it defines \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}(G)\), develops balanced SDPs, and proves that their symmetric/eigenvalue reductions do not improve on \(\mathrm{b}h(G)\), motivating the open problem of stronger spectral closed forms.

sources.title: On the Shannon capacity of a graph
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the Lovász theta number \(\vartheta(G)\), an SDP computable upper bound on \(\alpha(G)\) that is exact for bipartite/perfect graphs and underlies many spectral/SDP bounds.
sources.connection: The balanced SDPs in the source paper are explicit variations of \(\vartheta\); any improved closed-form eigenvalue bound for \(\alpha_{\mathrm{bal}}\) would likely be interpretable as a structured restriction/rounding of a theta-like SDP.

sources.title: Geometric Algorithms and Combinatorial Optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops the ellipsoid/SDP framework for \(\vartheta\) and related relaxations; establishes exactness of \(\vartheta\) on perfect graphs and foundational duality/structure used in spectral bounds.
sources.connection: Provides the canonical reference for theta/SDP duality and symmetry reduction arguments that the source paper uses when deriving eigenvalue closed forms; relevant for attempts to derive new structured feasible dual certificates tailored to balance.

sources.title: A comparison of the Sherali-Adams, Lovász-Schrijver and Lasserre relaxations for 0-1 programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Systematic comparison of lift-and-project/SOS hierarchies; shows convergence properties and relationships among relaxations for 0–1 problems, including stable set formulations.
sources.connection: Balanced independent set can be encoded by adding a linear constraint \(f^Tx=0\) to the stable-set ideal; this paper is a key reference for how such constraints interact with SOS/Lasserre levels and for understanding why low-level symmetric restrictions may be too coarse.

sources.title: An explicit exact SDP relaxation for nonlinear 0-1 programs
sources.bibtex:
@inproceedings{Lasserre2001Explicit,
  title     = {An explicit exact {SDP} relaxation for nonlinear 0--1 programs},
  author    = {Lasserre, Jean B.},
  booktitle = {Integer Programming and Combinatorial Optimization (IPCO)},
  series    = {Lecture Notes in Computer Science},
  volume    = {2081},
  pages     = {293--303},
  year      = {2001},
  publisher = {Springer},
  doi       = {10.1007/3-540-44676-1_20}
}
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces the Lasserre/SOS hierarchy giving increasingly tight SDP relaxations for polynomial 0–1 optimization, exact at sufficiently high level.
sources.connection: The balanced constraint \(f^Tx=0\) fits naturally into the SOS framework; stronger eigenvalue bounds might correspond to identifying low-degree SOS certificates specialized to regular bipartite graphs beyond the symmetric ansatz yielding \(\mathrm{b}h(G)\).

sources.title: Bicliques and eigenvalues
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops spectral parameters (e.g., \(\varphi\), \(\varphi'\), \(\varphi_H\)) to upper bound biclique-related quantities via eigenvalues; connects to complements and Laplacian spectra.
sources.connection: Although focused on bicliques, the source paper relates its \(h_1\)/\(\mathrm{b}h\) bounds to Haemers’ parameters through bipartite doubles/complements; techniques suggest alternative spectral certificates that might be adapted to balanced biindependent sets.

sources.title: Disconnected vertex sets and equidistant code pairs
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces spectral techniques for bounding sizes of disconnected vertex sets and related extremal configurations; precursor to later biclique/eigenvalue bounds.
sources.connection: Balanced biindependent pairs correspond to large disconnected bipartite substructures; Haemers’ framework is a potential source of alternative eigenvalue bounds beyond the \(\lambda_2\)-only expression \(\mathrm{b}h(G)\).

sources.title: Hoffman’s ratio bound
sources.theorem_or_result: Modern account and proof of Hoffman’s ratio bound \(\alpha(G)\le \frac{n(-\lambda_{\min})}{r-\lambda_{\min}}\) for \(r\)-regular graphs, and related spectral extremal methods.
sources.connection: Hoffman-type bounds are the archetype of closed-form eigenvalue bounds for independent sets; the open problem asks for an analog that better captures the balancing constraint in bipartite regular graphs, potentially involving both positive and negative spectrum or refined interlacing.
